task_completion.visual_evidence: An IDE (like PyCharm or VSCode) or terminal text editor (like nano/vim) is open, showing the contents of '/home/ga/PycharmProjects/pycal_importer/parser.py'.

task_completion.description: Modify the code to correctly handle iCalendar line unfolding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code changes in parser.py showing logic to unfold lines. Look for string replacements like `replace('\r\n ', '')` or `replace('\n ', '')`, or a loop that joins a line to the previous one if it starts with a space or tab, replacing a naive `splitlines()` implementation.

task_completion.description: Modify the code to correctly parse properties with parameters (e.g., handling timezones).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code changes in parser.py showing logic to handle parameters before the colon. Look for splitting logic that accounts for semicolons (e.g., `line.split(';', 1)` or regex) so that properties like 'DTSTART;TZID=...' are parsed correctly instead of naively splitting only on the first colon.

task_completion.description: Modify the code to handle date-only formats for all-day events.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code changes in parser.py showing conditional logic for date parsing. Look for checks on string length (e.g., `len(val) == 8`), handling of 'VALUE=DATE', or using `strptime` with just `%Y%m%d` alongside the standard datetime format.

task_completion.description: Execute the test suite to verify the fixes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing the execution of the test suite (e.g., running `pytest tests/` or `python -m unittest`). The output should display the test results, ideally showing tests passing.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the tests using a test runner.

integrity.description: The agent fixed the parsing logic generally, rather than hardcoding specific strings to pass the tests.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code changes in parser.py use general string manipulation, datetime parsing, or regex. There should be no `if` statements checking for exact test strings (e.g., `if line == 'DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:20231031T100000': return ...`).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is located at '/home/ga/PycharmProjects/pycal_importer'. The parser.py file contains three specific bugs that must be fixed: 1) 'line_unfolding' (naive splitlines failing on folded lines), 2) 'parameter_parsing' (naive splitting on the first colon failing on properties with parameters like TZID), and 3) 'date_only_parsing' (assuming a fixed datetime format, crashing on VALUE=DATE). The agent must fix these logic errors and run the tests in the tests/ directory. A passing score requires the fixes to be robust enough to pass both public tests and hidden stress tests (threshold: 80 points).
